Applied_Species.reason: Review explicitly discusses LPOR in all plants/algae/cyanobacteria and specifically mentions angiosperms, Arabidopsis, barley, and cyanobacterial examples.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly named; barley is mentioned but not by Latin name in the text; broader Viridiplantae wording is an inference.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper frames LPOR as biologically pivotal and mentions engineering-like possibilities only indirectly; recommendations about improving emergence, low-light performance, and context-specific tuning are extrapolations not stated in the review.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Strongly supported: LPOR promotes light-dependent Pchlide to Chlide conversion, chlorophyll accumulation, PLB dynamics, chloroplast maturation, and photomorphogenesis.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed evidence types are discussed: purified protein photocatalysis, spectroscopy in vivo/in vitro, Arabidopsis knockout complementation, structural biology, and pigment/metabolite analyses.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Dark-to-light transition, etiolation, visible light illumination, and low-light greening are described; oxygen relevance is discussed mainly in comparison to DPOR.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: No epigenetic regulation of LPOR is described in the provided text.
